subject: Science
note: Achievement objectives from the New Zealand Curriculum. Science has five strands: Nature of Science (NoS), Living World, Physical World, Material World, Planet Earth and Beyond.

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Observe, sort, and describe objects and materials by their properties using words, pictures, diagrams, and tables

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Ask questions and find simple explanations for the properties of everyday objects and materials

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Recognise that living things grow, reproduce, and have different features; identify that they have requirements to stay alive

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Explore everyday examples of physical phenomena, including light, sound, heat, motion, and electricity, and describe these simply

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Describe the properties of materials and simple changes in materials (e.g. mixing, heating, cooling)

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Explore and describe natural features and resources of the local environment; describe how the Sun, Earth, and Moon move relative to each other

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Extend their experiences and personal explanations of the natural world through exploration, play, asking questions, and discussing simple models

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Recognise that all living things have a life cycle; describe how living things are suited to their particular habitat and how they respond to environmental changes

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Explore and describe the effects of forces (e.g. push, pull, gravity) on objects and everyday uses of simple circuits and electrical energy

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Describe properties of materials that enable them to be used and transformed for specific purposes; distinguish physical from chemical changes

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Describe how natural processes have shaped the local landscape and how heat from the Sun affects the Earth

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Collect and use evidence in investigations; consider the reliability of data and develop more complex models to explain natural phenomena

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Identify the characteristics of living things; describe how adaptations of organisms suit different environments; understand food chains and webs

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Investigate and describe the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on objects; describe the properties and transfer of light, sound, and heat energy

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Classify substances as pure or impure; describe physical and chemical changes and investigate properties of common materials

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Describe the water cycle and its effects on environments; investigate features of the solar system

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Use scientific methodology (planning, data collection, analysis, conclusion) and communicate findings using appropriate scientific terminology and conventions

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Understand life processes at a cellular level: cell structure and function, photosynthesis, cellular respiration, reproduction

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Understand the basic principles of genetic inheritance and how natural selection drives evolution

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Describe forces and motion in terms of Newton's laws; investigate the properties of waves (light, sound) and electricity and magnetism

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Investigate chemical and physical changes; understand acids, bases, and neutralisation reactions; relate properties of materials to their structure

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Understand geological features of New Zealand (volcanoes, earthquakes, erosion) and the structure of the Earth

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Use and critique scientific models and theories to explain the natural world; evaluate the quality of scientific evidence and discuss ethical implications of scientific inquiry

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Understand cell biology (organelles, DNA, protein synthesis), genetics (inheritance, mutations), and mechanisms of evolution (natural and artificial selection)

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Apply physics concepts: mechanics (forces, energy, momentum), electricity and magnetism, waves (light, sound, electromagnetic spectrum)

objectives.subject: Science
objectives.text: Understand atomic structure, chemical bonding (ionic, covalent, metallic), types of chemical reactions, and the periodic table
